Diego buiiness license. The Ia$, practice side of my business is done
through a consulting arrangement with the above-referenced faw firn.
With respect to disclosure requirements, it is rny understanding

is ny law practice, ny spousers law practice, and my spousers salary as
a law school professor. Further, specific sources of income must be

requirements of being a source of j.ncome in excess of $1o,oo0 and doing
bus-iness in the juriiaiction 
(e.g. the LAIO 1aw firrn and other clients
neeting the threshold requirenents). I $rould appreciate confirmation
of this understandinq.
Wj.th regard to conflict of interest, I understand that I must

in a vote which would affect an applicant
who is also a source of income to my spouse or me. My questions are as

sections.question:
1.) What is my obligation when an applicant is known by me to be a client of LALO but is not a source of income to my spouse or me. That is, I have not derived any income from that particular client and only know it to be a client of the law firm?

2.) What is my responsibility regarding an applicant as described in #1 above who is represented before the Port Commission by a lawyer from LALO?

3.) What is my obligation to ascertain whether an applicant in whom I have no financial interest is a client of LALO?

4.) What if an applicant is a client of LALO unbeknown to me?

5.) What is my responsibility to know who my spouse's clients are? What if one appears before the Port, but I am unaware that he/she is a client of my spouse? What if a client of my spouse has a matter before the Port unknown to my spouse or me?

6.) What is my responsibility when a matter before the Port may have a direct or indirect impact upon a client from whom my spouse or I have received income but I am unaware that there would be such an impact?

7.) What is my responsibility in ascertaining potential direct or indirect impact on clients in every action coming before the Board of Port Commissioners? [Includes hypothetical regarding City of National City zoning change]

sections.question_synthetic: Lynn Schenk seeks clarification on her conflict of interest obligations as a San Diego Port Commissioner, specifically regarding: (1) disclosure requirements for sources of income from her law practice and spouse's income; (2) whether she must abstain from votes affecting LALO clients even when she has not personally derived income from them; and (3) the extent of her duty to ascertain whether applicants are clients of LALO or her spouse's practice.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: This is an incoming request/inquiry document, not an FPPC response letter. The document contains multiple copies/iterations of the memorandum with identical content. No formal FPPC conclusion or advice section is present. Some OCR quality issues in source material but key question sections are clearly legible. Requestor references previous telephone conversations with John Wallace at FPPC.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1.) What is my obligation when an applicant is known by me to be a client of LALO but is not a source of income to my spouse or me. That is, I have not derived any income from that particular client and only know it to be a client of the law firm?

2.) What is my responsibility regarding an applicant as described in #1 above who is represented before the Port Commission by a lawyer from LALO?

3.) What is my obligation to ascertain whether an applicant in whom I have no financial interest is a client of LALO?

4.) What if an applicant is a client of LALO unbeknown to me?

5.) What is my responsibility to know who my spouse's clients are? What if one appears before the Port, but I am unaware that he/she is a client of my spouse? What if a client of my spouse has a matter before the Port unknown to my spouse or me?

6.) What is my responsibility when a matter before the Port may have a direct or indirect impact upon a client from whom my spouse or I have received income but I am unaware that there would be such an impact?

7.) What is my responsibility in ascertaining potential direct or indirect impact on clients in every action coming before the Board of Port Commissioners? [Includes hypothetical regarding City of National City zoning change]

embedding.summary: Lynn Schenk, a San Diego Port Commissioner with a consulting arrangement with law firm LALO, submits a detailed written request to FPPC staff seeking guidance on conflict of interest regulations and disclosure requirements applicable to her appointed position, including multiple specific scenarios regarding applicants who may be clients of the law firm.
